source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Some shapes are drawn on a piece of paper. The teacher folds the left-hand side of the paper over the central bold line. How many of the shapes on the left-hand side will fit exactly on top of a shape on the right-hand side? <image1>

image_dependency: question_text explicitly asks about folding shapes over a central line and matching left-side shapes to right-side shapes
asset_registry_record.image_manifest shows a valid image exists and registry_passed=true
multi_step: solving requires identifying shapes on the left side, mentally reflecting them across the central line, and checking exact overlap with shapes on the right
question asks for a count after per-shape comparison rather than a single direct lookup
the task involves spatial reasoning about symmetry/reflection, not just reading a visible label
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single number ('3')
the required output is a discrete count, which is easier to check against the image than an open-ended explanation
registry_passed=true and the image quality summary indicates strong readability

decision_reasons: asset_registry_record shows complete core assets: question present, answer present, and valid primary image with no registry issues
initial_scoring_record indicates strong image dependency and high verifiability, matching the available image-based content
the task appears recoverable and well-supported for collection, with no evidence of missing core semantic assets
